title: Home
content: syncitron Documentation Enterprise-grade local-first synchronization framework for Flutter. Build offline-capable apps with automatic sync, conflict resolution, and comprehensive monitoring. 🔌 Pluggable Backends Supabase, Firebase, Appwrite, or any GraphQL server 📱 True Offline-First Full functionality offline, seamless sync when connected 🧠 Smart Conflicts ServerWins, LocalWins, LastWriteWins, or Custom strategies ⚡ Batch Operations 50-100× faster syncs — eliminates the N+1 problem 📊 Observability Structured logging, metrics, and health checks 🛡️ Error Recovery Sealed exception hierarchy with retry and backoff 🎨 Ready-made Widgets 6 Flutter widgets for sync status, errors, and metrics 📡 Real-Time Event-driven sync with all four backends Quick Install flutter pub add syncitron Minimal Example import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart'; import 'package:supabase_flutter/supabase_flutter.dart'; final engine = SyncEngine( localStore: SqfliteStore(database, conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace), remoteAdapter: SupabaseAdapter( client: supabaseClient, localStore: sqfliteStore, postgresChangeEventAll: PostgresChangeEvent.all, ), config: syncitronConfig.production(), logger: ConsoleLogger(), ); engine.registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'todos', columns: ['id', 'title', 'completed', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], strategy: SyncStrategy.lastWriteWins, )); await engine.init(); final metrics = await engine.syncAll(); print(metrics.overallSuccess); // true Documentation Map Section Contents Quick Start Install, configure, sync in 5 minutes Architecture Engine, stores, adapters, data flow Sync Engine Core API: init, syncAll, syncTable, registerTable Conflict Resolution 4 strategies with examples Configuration syncitronConfig presets and custom tuning Error Handling Sealed exception hierarchy Metrics & Logging SyncMetrics, Loggers, MetricsCollector Backends Supabase · Firebase · Appwrite · GraphQL Storage SQLite · Drift · Hive · Isar Orchestration 5 built-in sync strategies for advanced flows Real-Time Event-driven sync with auto-reconnect UI Widgets 6 ready-made Flutter widgets API Reference Complete class/method reference Next → Quick Start syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Quick Start
content: Quick Start Your first offline-first sync in 5 minutes Get your first offline-first sync running in under 5 minutes. On this page 1. Installation 2. Set Up Local Database 3. Create Sync Engine 4. Register Tables 5. Sync What's Next 1. Installation flutter pub add syncitron Or add manually to pubspec.yaml : dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 sqflite: ^2.4.2 # local store (user dependency) supabase_flutter: ^2.12.0 # remote backend (user dependency) ℹ️ syncitron has zero third-party dependencies . All backend packages (Supabase, Firebase, Appwrite, GraphQL) and local stores (sqflite, Drift, Hive, Isar) are optional — add only the ones you actually use to your own pubspec.yaml . 2. Set Up Local Database import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart'; import 'package:path/path.dart'; final db = await openDatabase( join(await getDatabasesPath(), 'myapp.db'), version: 1, onCreate: (db, version) async { await db.execute( 'CREATE TABLE todos (' ' id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,' ' title TEXT NOT NULL,' ' completed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,' ' updated_at TEXT,' ' deleted_at TEXT' ')' ); }, ); ℹ️ syncitron automatically adds is_synced and op_id columns via ensureSyncColumns() during engine.init() . You do not need to create them manually. 3. Create the Sync Engine import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart'; import 'package:supabase_flutter/supabase_flutter.dart'; final localStore = SqfliteStore(db, conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace); final remoteAdapter = SupabaseAdapter( client: Supabase.instance.client, localStore: localStore, postgresChangeEventAll: PostgresChangeEvent.all, isAuthException: (e) => e is AuthException, ); final engine = SyncEngine( localStore: localStore, remoteAdapter: remoteAdapter, config: syncitronConfig.production(), logger: ConsoleLogger(minLevel: LogLevel.info), metricsCollector: InMemoryMetricsCollector(), ); 4. Register Tables engine .registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'todos', columns: ['id', 'title', 'completed', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], strategy: SyncStrategy.lastWriteWins, )) .registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'projects', columns: ['id', 'name', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], strategy: SyncStrategy.serverWins, )); ⚠️ registerTable() must be called before engine.init() . The method returns the engine so you can chain calls. 5. Sync! await engine.init(); // idempotent — safe to call multiple times final metrics = await engine.syncAll(); // pull + push all registered tables print('Success: ${metrics.overallSuccess}'); print('Pulled: ${metrics.totalRecordsPulled}'); print('Pushed: ${metrics.totalRecordsPushed}'); What's Next Architecture Overview — understand the data flow Conflict Resolution — pick the right strategy Configuration — tune batch size, retries, timeouts UI Widgets — drop-in sync status & error widgets ← Previous Home Next → Architecture syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Architecture
content: Architecture How syncitron is structured syncitron follows a clean, layered architecture with dependency injection throughout. High-Level Data Flow ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Your Flutter App │ │ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌───────────────┐ │ │ │ UI Widgets │ │ Your Code │ │ State Mgmt │ │ │ └──────┬───────┘ └──────┬───────┘ └───────┬───────┘ │ │ └─────────────────┼──────────────────┘ │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌─────────────────── SyncEngine ───────────────────┐ │ │ │ registerTable() · syncAll() · syncTable() │ │ │ │ statusStream · conflict resolution · retry │ │ │ └────────────┬────────────────────┬────────────────┘ │ │ ▼ ▼ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────┐ │ │ │ LocalStore │ │ RemoteAdapter │ │ │ │ (sqflite/drift │ │ (supabase/firebase/ │ │ │ │ hive/isar) │ │ appwrite/graphql) │ │ │ └────────┬───────┘ └──────────┬──────────┘ │ │ ▼ ▼ │ │ Local SQLite/NoSQL Remote Backend │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ Core Components SyncEngine The central orchestrator. It coordinates pulling data from the remote backend, pushing local changes, resolving conflicts, and emitting status updates. All sync operations go through the engine. LocalStore (Abstract) Handles all local persistence: reading/writing sync cursors, querying dirty (unsynced) records, upserting batches, and marking records as synced. Four implementations are provided: Store Backing Best For SqfliteStore SQLite Production — battle-tested, 100K+ records DriftStore SQLite (typed) Type safety, reactive streams, code gen HiveStore Hive (NoSQL) Fast prototyping, small datasets IsarStore Isar (NoSQL) High-performance mobile, encryption RemoteAdapter (Abstract) Handles all remote communication: pulling pages of records, upserting, and soft deleting. Each adapter also provides optional real-time subscription support. Adapter Backend Real-Time SupabaseAdapter Supabase (PostgreSQL) ✅ via WebSocket LISTEN/NOTIFY FirebaseFirestoreAdapter Cloud Firestore ✅ via Snapshot listeners AppwriteAdapter Appwrite ✅ via RealtimeService GraphQLAdapter Any GraphQL server ✅ via Subscriptions syncitronConfig Immutable configuration controlling batch sizes, retry policies, timeouts, and feature flags. Three presets: production() , development() , testing() . TableConfig Per-table configuration specifying column mappings, sync strategy, and optional custom conflict resolver. Sync Cycle Init — engine.init() ensures sync columns ( is_synced , op_id ) exist in the local database. Pull — Fetches remote records modified since the last cursor, using keyset pagination. Conflicts are detected and resolved per the table's strategy. Push — Queries local dirty records ( is_synced = 0 ), assigns idempotent operation IDs, then batch-upserts/deletes to the remote backend. Mark Synced — Successfully pushed records are marked is_synced = 1 locally. Cursor Update — The sync cursor is advanced to the latest updated_at timestamp. ✅ All sync operations are idempotent . The operation ID ( op_id ) prevents duplicate writes even if the network fails mid-push and the engine retries. Dependency Graph SyncEngine ├── LocalStore (required) ├── RemoteAdapter (required) ├── syncitronConfig (optional — defaults to syncitronConfig()) ├── Logger (optional — defaults to ConsoleLogger()) └── MetricsCollector (optional — defaults to InMemoryMetricsCollector()) ← Previous Quick Start Next → Sync Engine syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Sync Engine
content: Sync Engine Core orchestrator for all sync operations The SyncEngine is the central class of syncitron. It orchestrates pulling, pushing, conflict resolution, metrics, and status updates. Constructor SyncEngine({ required LocalStore localStore, required RemoteAdapter remoteAdapter, syncitronConfig? config, // default: syncitronConfig() Logger? logger, // default: ConsoleLogger() MetricsCollector? metricsCollector, // default: InMemoryMetricsCollector() }) Public API Method Returns Description init() Future<void> Ensures sync columns exist. Idempotent — safe to call repeatedly. registerTable(TableConfig) SyncEngine Registers a table. Returns this for chaining. Must be called before init() . syncAll() Future<SyncSessionMetrics> Pull + push all registered tables. Skips if sync is already running. syncTable(TableConfig) Future<SyncMetrics> Sync a single table by config. Skips if sync is already running. syncTableByName(String) Future<SyncMetrics> Sync a single table by name. Looks up the registered TableConfig. syncWithOrchestration(strategy) Future<SyncSessionMetrics> Execute a custom SyncOrchestrationStrategy . dispose() void Closes the status stream controller. Call on app shutdown. statusStream A broadcast Stream<String> emitting human-readable status messages during sync. Wire it to SyncStatusWidget or listen directly: engine.statusStream.listen((msg) => print('Sync: $msg')); // "Starting Full Sync..." // "Downloading todos..." // "Uploading todos..." // "Sync completed successfully." Overlap Protection If syncAll() or syncTable() is called while a sync is already in progress, the call returns immediately with empty metrics and a warning is logged. This prevents concurrent writes to the local database. Error Tolerance syncAll() does not abort on a single table failure. If table A fails, table B is still synced. Errors are captured in SyncSessionMetrics.totalErrors . final metrics = await engine.syncAll(); if (!metrics.overallSuccess) { for (final tm in metrics.tableMetrics) { if (!tm.success) print('Failed: ${tm.tableName} — ${tm.errorMessages}'); } } ← Previous Architecture Next → Conflict Resolution syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Conflict Resolution
content: Conflict Resolution ServerWins · LocalWins · LastWriteWins · Custom A conflict occurs when a record was modified both locally and remotely since the last sync. syncitron supports four strategies, configured per table. SyncStrategy Enum Strategy Behaviour Use Case serverWins Remote record overwrites local. Default. Reference data, admin settings localWins Local record is kept; remote update ignored. Drafts, user preferences lastWriteWins Record with the latest updated_at wins. Collaborative content custom Your custom resolver function runs. Complex merge logic Setting the Strategy // Per table engine.registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'settings', columns: ['id', 'key', 'value', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], strategy: SyncStrategy.serverWins, )); Custom Conflict Resolver When SyncStrategy.custom is selected, you must provide a customResolver . The resolver receives both records and returns a ConflictResolution . engine.registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'documents', columns: ['id', 'title', 'body', 'version', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], strategy: SyncStrategy.custom, customResolver: (local, remote) async { // Keep the one with the higher version if ((local['version'] as int) >= (remote['version'] as int)) { return const UseLocal(); } return UseRemote(remote); }, )); ConflictResolution Types Type Meaning UseLocal() Keep the local dirty record as-is. UseRemote(data) Overwrite local with the remote record data. UseMerged(data) Save a manually merged map combining both versions. 🚫 Using SyncStrategy.custom without providing a customResolver will throw an EngineConfigurationException at registration time. How Conflicts Are Detected During the pull phase, for each incoming remote record, the engine checks if a local record with the same primary key exists and has is_synced = 0 (dirty). If so, it's a conflict. If the local record is already synced, the remote version is simply upserted. ← Previous Sync Engine Next → Configuration syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Configuration
content: Configuration syncitronConfig — presets and tuning syncitronConfig is an immutable class controlling engine behaviour. Three factory constructors provide sensible presets. Presets syncitronConfig.production() // large batches, aggressive retries syncitronConfig.development() // small batches, detailed logging syncitronConfig.testing() // minimal overhead, no logging/metrics All Parameters Parameter Type Default Description batchSize int 500 Max records per pull page / push batch maxConcurrentSyncs int 1 Reserved for future parallel sync operationTimeout Duration 30 s Timeout per remote operation maxRetries int 3 Max retry attempts on failure initialRetryDelay Duration 300 ms First retry wait time maxRetryDelay Duration 30 s Cap for exponential backoff isSyncedColumn String 'is_synced' Local column tracking sync status operationIdColumn String 'op_id' Local column for idempotency keys autoSyncOnStartup bool false Auto-trigger syncAll after init periodicSyncInterval Duration? null If set, enables periodic sync timer enableDetailedLogging bool false Log individual record operations collectMetrics bool true Whether to record sync metrics validateOnCreation bool true Run validate() in constructor Custom Configuration final config = syncitronConfig( batchSize: 1000, maxRetries: 5, initialRetryDelay: Duration(seconds: 1), maxRetryDelay: Duration(minutes: 5), enableDetailedLogging: false, periodicSyncInterval: Duration(minutes: 10), ); copyWith Create a modified copy without mutating the original: final custom = syncitronConfig.production().copyWith( batchSize: 2000, enableDetailedLogging: true, ); Validation validate() is called automatically during construction (unless validateOnCreation: false ). It checks: batchSize > 0 maxRetries >= 0 initialRetryDelay > 0 maxRetryDelay >= initialRetryDelay ← Previous Conflict Resolution Next → Error Handling syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Error Handling
content: Error Handling Sealed exception hierarchy syncitron uses a sealed exception hierarchy rooted at syncitronException . You can pattern-match on specific types for granular error handling. Exception Hierarchy syncitronException (sealed) ├── SyncNetworkException — network / timeout / server error ├── SyncAuthException — 401/403, session expired ├── ConflictResolutionException — custom resolver threw ├── SchemaMigrationException — ALTER TABLE failed ├── LocalStoreException — local DB read/write error ├── UnregisteredTableException — table not registered └── EngineConfigurationException — invalid config Pattern Matching try { await engine.syncAll(); } on SyncNetworkException catch (e) { if (e.isOffline) showOfflineBanner(); else showError('Server error: ${e.statusCode}'); } on SyncAuthException catch (e) { redirectToLogin(); } on ConflictResolutionException catch (e) { log('Conflict on ${e.table} pk=${e.primaryKey}'); } on SchemaMigrationException catch (e) { reportFatalError(e); } on LocalStoreException catch (e) { showError('Database error: ${e.message}'); } on syncitronException catch (e) { // catch-all for any syncitron error showError('Sync error: ${e.message}'); } Exception Details SyncNetworkException Field Type Description table String The table being synced statusCode int? HTTP status code, null if offline isOffline bool Getter — true when statusCode is null cause Object? Original underlying exception SyncAuthException Field Type Description table String The table being synced message String Default: 'Unauthorized. Session may have expired.' ConflictResolutionException Field Type Description table String Table where conflict occurred primaryKey dynamic Primary key of the conflicting record cause Object? The error thrown by the custom resolver SchemaMigrationException Field Type Description table String Table being migrated column String Column that failed to add LocalStoreException Field Type Description table String Table with the local DB error message String Error description UnregisteredTableException Thrown when you attempt to sync a table that hasn't been registered via registerTable() . EngineConfigurationException Thrown at setup time, e.g. using SyncStrategy.custom without a customResolver , or invalid config values. ℹ️ All exceptions include a cause field for chaining the original underlying error. ← Previous Configuration Next → Metrics & Logging syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Metrics & Logging
content: Metrics & Logging Track performance and debug sync flows Sync Metrics Every syncAll() returns a SyncSessionMetrics containing per-table SyncMetrics . SyncSessionMetrics Property Type Description totalDuration Duration Total wall time for the session totalTablesSynced int Number of tables processed totalRecordsPulled int Sum of all pulled records totalRecordsPushed int Sum of all pushed records totalConflicts int Sum of conflicts encountered totalErrors int Number of failed table syncs overallSuccess bool true if all tables synced without errors tableMetrics List<SyncMetrics> Per-table breakdown SyncMetrics (per table) Property Type tableName String duration Duration recordsPulled int recordsPushed int recordsWithConflicts int conflictsResolved int errors int success bool Usage final metrics = await engine.syncAll(); print(metrics); // pretty-printed summary print(metrics.toJson()); // structured JSON for (final tm in metrics.tableMetrics) { if (!tm.success) { print('${tm.tableName} failed: ${tm.errorMessages}'); } } MetricsCollector Implementation Behaviour InMemoryMetricsCollector Stores all sessions and table metrics in memory. Useful for dashboards. NoOpMetricsCollector Discards all metrics. Zero overhead for production. Logging syncitron provides a pluggable Logger interface with three built-in implementations. Logger Implementations Class Behaviour ConsoleLogger Prints to stdout . Accepts minLevel to filter. NoOpLogger Discards all log output. Zero overhead. MultiLogger Fan-out: delegates to a list of loggers. Log Levels Level Severity LogLevel.debug 0 — Verbose debugging LogLevel.info 1 — Normal operations LogLevel.warning 2 — Potential issues LogLevel.error 3 — Errors requiring attention LogLevel.critical 4 — Fatal errors Custom Logger Integration class SentryLogger implements Logger { @override void error(String msg, {Object? error, StackTrace? stackTrace, Map<String, dynamic>? context}) { Sentry.captureException(error, stackTrace: stackTrace); } // ... implement other methods } final engine = SyncEngine( localStore: store, remoteAdapter: adapter, logger: MultiLogger([ConsoleLogger(), SentryLogger()]), ); LogEntry Structured log entries can be serialized to JSON for export: final entry = LogEntry( level: LogLevel.error, message: 'Sync failed', error: exception, context: {'table': 'todos', 'records': 42}, ); print(entry.toJson()); ← Previous Error Handling Next → Supabase syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Supabase
content: Supabase Backend PostgreSQL via Supabase + real-time WebSocket sync SupabaseAdapter connects syncitron to a Supabase PostgreSQL backend. It supports cursor-based pagination, native batch upserts, and real-time subscriptions via WebSocket. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 supabase_flutter: ^2.12.0 sqflite: ^2.4.2 import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; import 'package:supabase_flutter/supabase_flutter.dart'; final adapter = SupabaseAdapter( client: Supabase.instance.client, localStore: sqfliteStore, postgresChangeEventAll: PostgresChangeEvent.all, isAuthException: (e) => e is AuthException, updatedAtColumn: 'updated_at', // default ); Required Database Schema Every table synced with syncitron must have these columns on Supabase: CREATE TABLE todos ( id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), title TEXT NOT NULL, completed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, -- Required by syncitron: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL ); -- Recommended: index for keyset pagination performance CREATE INDEX idx_todos_updated_at ON todos(updated_at); ⚠️ The updated_at column must be auto-updated on every write. Use a Supabase trigger or set it in your application code. Real-Time Subscriptions final realtimeProvider = SupabaseRealtimeProvider( client: Supabase.instance.client, postgresChangeEventAll: PostgresChangeEvent.all, connectionTimeout: Duration(seconds: 30), ); final manager = RealtimeSubscriptionManager( config: RealtimeSubscriptionConfig.production(), provider: realtimeProvider, engine: engine, logger: ConsoleLogger(), ); await manager.initialize(['todos', 'projects']); Batch Operations SupabaseAdapter uses native PostgreSQL UPSERT for batch operations — true atomic writes for maximum throughput. Security Enable Row-Level Security (RLS) on all synced tables Handle session expiry: catch SyncAuthException and re-authenticate Never log auth tokens — use NoOpLogger in production if needed ← Previous Metrics & Logging Next → Firebase Firestore syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Firebase Firestore
content: Firebase Firestore Backend Cloud Firestore with real-time snapshot listeners FirebaseFirestoreAdapter connects to Cloud Firestore. Uses dynamic typing to avoid hard dependencies — add cloud_firestore to your pubspec. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 firebase_core: ^2.24.0 cloud_firestore: ^4.13.0 import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final adapter = FirebaseFirestoreAdapter( firestore: FirebaseFirestore.instance, localStore: sqfliteStore, timeout: Duration(seconds: 30), enableOfflinePersistence: true, ); Constructor Parameter Type Default Description firestore dynamic required FirebaseFirestore instance localStore dynamic required Your LocalStore timeout Duration 30 s Per-operation timeout enableOfflinePersistence bool true Enable Firestore offline cache Additional Methods Method Description watchCollection(table) Returns a Stream of live collection snapshots batchWrite(operations) Execute multiple writes as a Firestore batch runTransaction(callback) Execute inside a Firestore transaction Real-Time final realtimeProvider = FirebaseFirestoreRealtimeProvider( firestore: FirebaseFirestore.instance, ); Batch Writes Uses Firestore's native batch API (up to 500 operations per batch). Automatically chunks larger batches. ← Previous Supabase Next → Appwrite syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Appwrite
content: Appwrite Backend Self-hosted BaaS with WebSocket real-time AppwriteAdapter connects to self-hosted or cloud Appwrite. Uses dynamic typing — add the appwrite package. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 appwrite: ^11.0.0 import 'package:appwrite/appwrite.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final client = Client() ..setEndpoint('https://cloud.appwrite.io/v1') ..setProject('your-project-id'); final adapter = AppwriteAdapter( client: client, database: Databases(client), localStore: sqfliteStore, databaseId: 'your-database-id', ); Constructor Parameter Type Default Description client dynamic required Appwrite Client database dynamic required Appwrite Databases instance localStore dynamic required Your LocalStore databaseId String required Appwrite database ID timeout Duration 30 s Per-operation timeout Additional Methods Method Description executeFunction(functionId, data) Execute an Appwrite Cloud Function batchWrite(table, creates, updates, deletes) Parallel batch operations watchCollection(table) Stream of live document changes Real-Time final realtimeProvider = AppwriteRealtimeProvider( client: client, databaseId: 'your-database-id', ); ← Previous Firebase Firestore Next → GraphQL syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: GraphQL
content: GraphQL Backend Works with Hasura, Apollo, or any GraphQL server GraphQLAdapter works with any GraphQL server (Hasura, Apollo, Supabase GraphQL, custom). You provide query/mutation builders; syncitron handles the sync logic. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 graphql: ^5.1.0 import 'package:graphql/client.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final graphqlClient = GraphQLClient( link: HttpLink('https://your-server.com/graphql'), cache: GraphQLCache(), ); final adapter = GraphQLAdapter( graphqlClient: graphqlClient, localStore: sqfliteStore, queryBuilder: (request) { return ''' query Pull(\$cursor: timestamptz, \$limit: Int!) { ${request.table}( where: {updated_at: {_gte: \$cursor}} order_by: {updated_at: asc} limit: \$limit ) { ${request.columns.join(' ')} } } '''; }, mutationBuilder: (table, data) { return ''' mutation Upsert(\$object: ${table}_insert_input!) { insert_${table}_one( object: \$object on_conflict: {constraint: ${table}_pkey, update_columns: [${data.keys.join(', ')}]} ) { id } } '''; }, ); Constructor Parameter Type Description graphqlClient dynamic GraphQL client instance localStore dynamic Your LocalStore queryBuilder Function(PullRequest) → String Builds the pull query mutationBuilder Function(String, Map) → String Builds upsert mutations softDeleteMutationBuilder Function(String, String) → String Builds soft-delete mutations timeout Duration Per-operation timeout (default 30s) Real-Time via Subscriptions final realtimeProvider = GraphQLRealtimeProvider( graphqlClient: graphqlClient, subscriptionQueryBuilder: (table) { return ''' subscription Watch { $table(order_by: {updated_at: desc}, limit: 1) { id updated_at } } '''; }, ); ← Previous Appwrite Next → SQLite (sqflite) syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: SQLite (sqflite)
content: SQLite Storage (sqflite) Recommended — production-proven SQLite storage SqfliteStore is the recommended local store. Battle-tested, suitable for 100K+ records, lowest memory footprint. Setup import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final db = await openDatabase('myapp.db', version: 1, onCreate: (db, v) async { await db.execute('CREATE TABLE todos (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT, ...)'); }); final store = SqfliteStore( db, conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace, // required isSyncedColumn: 'is_synced', // default operationIdColumn: 'op_id', // default ); Automatic Column Migration ensureSyncColumns() (called during engine.init() ) checks if is_synced and op_id columns exist and adds them via ALTER TABLE if missing. Cursor Persistence Sync cursors are stored in a private _syncitron_meta table, created automatically. This table maps table names to their last-synced updated_at + primaryKey pair. Batch Optimisations SQL parameter chunking: batches are split into chunks of 999 parameters to respect SQLite's SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER limit. markManyAsSynced() uses UPDATE … WHERE pk IN (...) instead of N individual updates. upsertBatch() uses INSERT OR REPLACE inside a single transaction. ← Previous GraphQL Next → Drift syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Drift
content: Drift Storage Type-safe SQL with compile-time safety DriftStore wraps a Drift (typed SQL) database. Schema management is compile-time, so ensureSyncColumns() is a no-op — ensure your Drift schema includes is_synced and op_id columns. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 drift: ^2.14.0 sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.5.0 import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final store = DriftStore( tables: {'todos': todosTable, 'projects': projectsTable}, readMetadataQuery: (table) => metadataDao.read(table), writeMetadataQuery: (table, json) => metadataDao.write(table, json), deleteMetadataQuery: (table) => metadataDao.delete(table), ); Constructor Parameter Type Description tables Map<String, dynamic> Map of table name → Drift table reference readMetadataQuery Function(String) Reads sync cursor JSON for a table writeMetadataQuery Function(String, String) Writes sync cursor JSON deleteMetadataQuery Function(String) Deletes sync cursor isSyncedColumn String Column name (default: 'is_synced' ) operationIdColumn String Column name (default: 'op_id' ) ⚠️ Your Drift schema must include is_synced (int) and op_id (text) columns. syncitron cannot add them at runtime. ← Previous SQLite (sqflite) Next → Hive syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Hive
content: Hive Storage Lightweight NoSQL — zero native dependencies HiveStore uses Hive boxes for lightweight NoSQL storage. Schema-less — great for rapid prototyping. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 hive_flutter: ^1.1.0 import 'package:hive_flutter/hive_flutter.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; await Hive.initFlutter(); final metadataBox = await Hive.openBox('syncitron_meta'); final store = HiveStore( metadataBox: metadataBox, dataBoxFactory: (table) => Hive.openBox(table), ); Constructor Parameter Type Description metadataBox dynamic Hive box for sync cursor storage dataBoxFactory Function(String) Factory returning the Hive box for a table name isSyncedColumn String Key name (default: 'is_synced' ) operationIdColumn String Key name (default: 'op_id' ) ℹ️ Hive is schema-less: ensureSyncColumns() is a no-op. The is_synced and op_id fields are simply added as keys in each record map. ← Previous Drift Next → Isar syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Isar
content: Isar Storage Rust-backed high-performance embedded database IsarStore uses Isar, a high-performance embedded NoSQL database backed by Rust. Ideal for mobile with encryption and indexing support. Setup dependencies: syncitron: ^0.5.1 isar: ^3.1.0 isar_flutter_libs: ^3.1.0 import 'package:isar/isar.dart'; import 'package:syncitron/syncitron.dart'; final isar = await Isar.open([TodoSchema, ProjectSchema]); final store = IsarStore( isar: isar, collectionFactory: (table) => isar.collection<dynamic>(table), ); Constructor Parameter Type Description isar dynamic Isar database instance collectionFactory Function(String) Returns the Isar collection for a table name isSyncedColumn String Property name (default: 'is_synced' ) operationIdColumn String Property name (default: 'op_id' ) ⚠️ Isar schemas are defined at compile time. Ensure your @collection classes include isSynced and opId properties. ← Previous Hive Next → Sync Orchestration syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Sync Orchestration
content: Sync Orchestration 5 built-in strategies + custom orchestration For advanced sync flows beyond the default pull-push pattern, syncitron provides SyncOrchestrationStrategy — a pluggable lifecycle for custom sync orchestrations. How It Works final metrics = await engine.syncWithOrchestration( OfflineFirstSyncOrchestration(), ); The engine creates a SyncOrchestrationContext and calls: strategy.beforeSync(context) — setup, logging, pre-checks strategy.execute(context) — the actual sync logic (must return SyncSessionMetrics ) strategy.afterSync(context, metrics) — cleanup, notifications SyncOrchestrationContext The context provides controlled access to engine internals: Member Description logger The engine's Logger metricsCollector The engine's MetricsCollector tableNames List of registered table names startTime When the sync started managedSyncTable(name) Sync a single table by name, returns SyncMetrics managedSyncAll() Sync all tables, returns SyncSessionMetrics shouldContinue() Returns false if cancelled cancel() Cancels the orchestration Built-in Strategies StandardSyncOrchestration Default pull-push for all tables. Same as syncAll() . OfflineFirstSyncOrchestration Graceful degradation: continues syncing remaining tables even after network errors. Stops after maxNetworkErrors consecutive failures. OfflineFirstSyncOrchestration(maxNetworkErrors: 3) StrictManualOrchestration Strict error handling — aborts on first failure. Useful for critical data. PrioritySyncOrchestration Syncs tables in priority order (higher number = synced first). PrioritySyncOrchestration({ 'user_settings': 100, // synced first 'todos': 50, 'analytics': 10, // synced last }) CompositeSyncOrchestration Chains multiple strategies sequentially. CompositeSyncOrchestration([ PrioritySyncOrchestration({'settings': 100}), StandardSyncOrchestration(), ]) Custom Strategy class MyStrategy extends SyncOrchestrationStrategy { @override Future<SyncSessionMetrics> execute(SyncOrchestrationContext ctx) async { final session = SyncSessionMetrics(); // Sync critical tables first for (final table in ['users', 'settings']) { final m = await ctx.managedSyncTable(table); session.addTableMetrics(m); if (!ctx.shouldContinue()) break; } // Then sync the rest for (final table in ctx.tableNames) { if (['users', 'settings'].contains(table)) continue; final m = await ctx.managedSyncTable(table); session.addTableMetrics(m); } session.endTime = DateTime.now().toUtc(); return session; } } ← Previous Isar Next → Real-Time Sync syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Real-Time Sync
content: Real-Time Sync Event-driven sync with auto-reconnect syncitron supports event-driven real-time synchronization for all four backends. When data changes on the server, the client syncs automatically — no polling needed. Architecture Remote Backend ──▶ RealtimeSubscriptionProvider ──▶ RealtimeSubscriptionManager (per backend) (debounce → engine.syncTableByName()) Setup final manager = RealtimeSubscriptionManager( config: RealtimeSubscriptionConfig.production(), provider: SupabaseRealtimeProvider( client: supabaseClient, postgresChangeEventAll: PostgresChangeEvent.all, ), engine: engine, logger: ConsoleLogger(), ); await manager.initialize(['todos', 'projects']); RealtimeSubscriptionConfig Parameter Type Default Description enabled bool true Master switch autoSync bool true Auto-sync when changes detected debounce Duration 2 s Debounce window to coalesce rapid changes connectionTimeout Duration 30 s Connection timeout autoReconnect bool true Auto-reconnect on disconnect maxReconnectAttempts int 5 Max reconnect tries backoffMultiplier double 2.0 Exponential backoff factor Presets RealtimeSubscriptionConfig.production() // 2s debounce, 5 retries, 2× backoff RealtimeSubscriptionConfig.development() // 1s debounce, 10 retries, 1.5× backoff RealtimeSubscriptionConfig.disabled() // everything off RealtimeSubscriptionManager API Method Description initialize(tables) Subscribe to listed tables, start listening syncTable(table) Manually queue a table for debounced sync syncPendingTables() Immediately sync all pending tables isConnected Getter — current connection status activeSubscriptionCount Number of active subscriptions subscribedTables List of subscribed table names close() Cancel all subscriptions and close connection Backend Support Matrix Backend Provider Class Protocol Supabase SupabaseRealtimeProvider PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY via WebSocket Firebase FirebaseFirestoreRealtimeProvider Firestore snapshot listeners Appwrite AppwriteRealtimeProvider WebSocket real-time service GraphQL GraphQLRealtimeProvider GraphQL subscriptions over WebSocket How It Works The provider subscribes to the backend's real-time channel for each table. When a RealtimeChangeEvent arrives (insert/update/delete), the table is added to the pending set. A debounce timer coalesces rapid changes (default 2 seconds). After the debounce window, engine.syncTableByName(table) is called for each pending table. On disconnect, auto-reconnect kicks in with exponential backoff. ← Previous Sync Orchestration Next → UI Widgets syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: UI Widgets
content: UI Widgets 6 ready-made Flutter widgets for sync UX syncitron ships 6 ready-made Flutter widgets for displaying sync status, errors, and metrics. SyncStatusWidget Displays sync status from the engine's statusStream . SyncStatusWidget( statusStream: engine.statusStream, onSync: () => engine.syncAll(), showProgress: true, builder: (context, status) => Text(status), // optional custom builder ) Prop Type Default Description statusStream Stream<String> required Status messages from engine onSync VoidCallback required Sync trigger callback builder Widget Function(…)? null Custom builder, overrides default UI showProgress bool true Show spinner during sync textColor Color? null Override text color SyncMetricsCard Shows sync performance metrics in a card layout. SyncMetricsCard( metrics: sessionMetrics, elevation: 2, backgroundColor: Colors.white, ) Prop Type Default metrics SyncSessionMetrics required elevation double 1 backgroundColor Color? null showWhenEmpty bool true SyncErrorBanner Context-aware error banner using Dart 3 pattern matching on the sealed syncitronException hierarchy. SyncErrorBanner( error: currentError, // syncitronException? onRetry: () => engine.syncAll(), onDismiss: () => setState(() => currentError = null), customMessage: null, // optional override text ) Prop Type Default error syncitronException? required onRetry VoidCallback? null onDismiss VoidCallback? null customMessage String? null When error is null , the banner is hidden (renders SizedBox.shrink() ). OfflineIndicator Small chip showing online/offline state. OfflineIndicator( isOnline: connectivityStatus, offlineIcon: Icons.cloud_off, onlineIcon: Icons.cloud_done, offlineLabel: 'Offline', ) SyncButton A FilledButton.icon that auto-disables and shows a spinner while syncing. SyncButton( onPressed: () => engine.syncAll(), isSyncing: isSyncInProgress, label: 'Sync Now', icon: Icons.sync, ) Prop Type Default onPressed VoidCallback required isSyncing bool required label String 'Sync' icon IconData Icons.sync SyncStatusPanel All-in-one dashboard combining status, metrics, errors, and sync button. SyncStatusPanel( statusStream: engine.statusStream, onSync: () => engine.syncAll(), metrics: lastSessionMetrics, error: currentError, onErrorDismiss: () => setState(() => currentError = null), showMetrics: true, showButton: true, showStatus: true, ) Prop Type Default statusStream Stream<String> required onSync VoidCallback required metrics SyncSessionMetrics? null error syncitronException? null onErrorDismiss VoidCallback? null showMetrics bool true showButton bool true showStatus bool true ← Previous Real-Time Sync Next → Multi-Engine syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Multi-Engine
content: Multi-Engine (SyncManager) Coordinate multiple SyncEngine instances SyncManager coordinates multiple SyncEngine instances — useful for multi-tenant apps, workspaces, or isolated sync contexts. Setup final manager = SyncManager( logger: ConsoleLogger(), metricsCollector: InMemoryMetricsCollector(), ); manager.registerEngine('workspace-a', engineA); manager.registerEngine('workspace-b', engineB); await manager.initializeAll(); API Method Returns Description registerEngine(id, engine) void Register an engine with a unique ID getEngine(id) SyncEngine? Retrieve an engine by ID getAllEngines() List<SyncEngine> All registered engines initializeAll() Future<void> Init all engines syncAll() Future<Map<String, SyncSessionMetrics>> Sync all engines, returns metrics keyed by ID syncEngine(id) Future<SyncSessionMetrics> Sync a specific engine by ID startPeriodicSync(interval:) void Start a periodic timer syncing all engines stopPeriodicSync() void Stop the periodic timer checkAllHealth() Future<Map<String, HealthCheckResult>> Health check all engines dispose() Future<void> Stop timers, dispose all engines Usage Pattern // Sync all engines final results = await manager.syncAll(); for (final entry in results.entries) { print('${entry.key}: ${entry.value.overallSuccess}'); } // Periodic sync every 5 minutes manager.startPeriodicSync(interval: Duration(minutes: 5)); // Cleanup await manager.dispose(); ← Previous UI Widgets Next → Health & Diagnostics syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Health & Diagnostics
content: Health & Diagnostics Monitor sync infrastructure health syncitron provides a diagnostics subsystem for monitoring the health of your sync infrastructure. HealthCheckResult HealthCheckResult( component: 'Database', status: HealthStatus.healthy, message: 'Connection is alive', details: {'tables': 5, 'total_rows': 1234}, ) Field Type Description component String Name of the component status HealthStatus healthy / degraded / unhealthy message String Human-readable status message details Map? Optional structured data timestamp DateTime Auto-set to UTC now HealthStatus Enum Value Meaning healthy All systems operational degraded Partial issues (e.g. slow sync, outdated data) unhealthy Critical failure SystemHealth Aggregates multiple HealthCheckResult s: final health = SystemHealth(results: { 'db': dbCheck, 'sync': syncCheck, }); print(health.isHealthy); // true if ALL healthy print(health.overallStatus); // worst status among all checks print(health.toJson()); // structured JSON Built-in Providers DatabaseDiagnosticsProvider Checks SQLite connectivity and returns table/row counts. final dbDiag = DatabaseDiagnosticsProvider(database); final result = await dbDiag.checkHealth(); final info = await dbDiag.getDiagnostics(); // {'database_type': 'SQLite', 'tables': 5, 'total_rows': 1234, ...} SyncDiagnosticsProvider Reports on the last sync status (time, success, metrics). final syncDiag = SyncDiagnosticsProvider( lastSyncSuccessful: true, lastSyncTime: DateTime.now(), lastSyncMetrics: metrics.toJson(), ); SystemDiagnosticsProvider Combines multiple providers into a single health check: final systemDiag = SystemDiagnosticsProvider([dbDiag, syncDiag]); final health = await systemDiag.checkHealth(); // Overall status: worst of all sub-checks ← Previous Multi-Engine Next → Testing syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Testing
content: Testing Dependency injection for fully testable sync syncitron's architecture is fully testable via dependency injection. All core interfaces have in-memory or mock implementations. Test Configuration final config = syncitronConfig.testing(); // Minimal overhead: no logging, no metrics, fast timeouts Test Doubles Strategy Interface Test Double LocalStore In-memory map-based implementation RemoteAdapter Fake with configurable responses Logger NoOpLogger() or mock that captures calls MetricsCollector InMemoryMetricsCollector or NoOpMetricsCollector Example: In-Memory Local Store class InMemoryLocalStore implements LocalStore { final Map<String, List<Map<String, dynamic>>> _tables = {}; final Map<String, String> _cursors = {}; List<Map<String, dynamic>> table(String name) => _tables.putIfAbsent(name, () => []); @override Future<List<Map<String, dynamic>>> queryDirty(String table) async => this.table(table).where((r) => r['is_synced'] == 0).toList(); @override Future<void> upsertBatch(String table, List<Map<String, dynamic>> records) async { for (final record in records) { final idx = this.table(table).indexWhere((r) => r['id'] == record['id']); if (idx >= 0) this.table(table)[idx] = record; else this.table(table).add(record); } } // ... implement remaining LocalStore methods } Example: Fake Remote Adapter class FakeRemoteAdapter extends RemoteAdapter { final Map<String, List<Map<String, dynamic>>> remoteTables = {}; final List<Map<String, dynamic>> upserts = []; @override Future<PullResult> pull(PullRequest request) async { final records = remoteTables[request.table] ?? []; return PullResult(records: records, nextCursor: null); } @override Future<void> upsert({ required String table, required Map<String, dynamic> data, String? idempotencyKey, }) async { upserts.add({'table': table, 'data': data, 'op_id': idempotencyKey}); } // ... implement remaining RemoteAdapter methods } Integration Test test('full sync cycle', () async { final local = InMemoryLocalStore(); final remote = FakeRemoteAdapter(); final engine = SyncEngine( localStore: local, remoteAdapter: remote, config: syncitronConfig.testing(), logger: NoOpLogger(), ); engine.registerTable(TableConfig( name: 'todos', columns: ['id', 'title', 'updated_at', 'deleted_at'], )); await engine.init(); final metrics = await engine.syncAll(); expect(metrics.overallSuccess, isTrue); }); ← Previous Health & Diagnostics Next → API Reference syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: API Reference
content: API Reference Complete class and method reference Complete class and method reference for syncitron v0.5.1. Organized by module. Modules Core — SyncEngine, syncitronConfig, TableConfig, Models Strategy & Conflict — SyncStrategy, ConflictResolution, Orchestration Exceptions — Sealed exception hierarchy Logging & Metrics — Logger, MetricsCollector, LogEntry Remote Adapters — Supabase, Firebase, Appwrite, GraphQL Local Stores — Sqflite, Drift, Hive, Isar Real-Time — Subscription config, providers, manager UI Widgets — 6 Flutter widgets Multi-Engine — SyncManager Diagnostics — Health checks Utilities — retry() Core SyncEngine SyncEngine({ required LocalStore localStore, required RemoteAdapter remoteAdapter, syncitronConfig? config, Logger? logger, MetricsCollector? metricsCollector, }) Method Signature init Future<void> init() registerTable SyncEngine registerTable(TableConfig config) syncAll Future<SyncSessionMetrics> syncAll() syncTable Future<SyncMetrics> syncTable(TableConfig config) syncTableByName Future<SyncMetrics> syncTableByName(String name) syncWithOrchestration Future<SyncSessionMetrics> syncWithOrchestration(SyncOrchestrationStrategy) dispose void dispose() statusStream Stream<String> get statusStream syncitronConfig const syncitronConfig({ int batchSize = 500, int maxConcurrentSyncs = 1, Duration operationTimeout = const Duration(seconds: 30), int maxRetries = 3, Duration initialRetryDelay = const Duration(milliseconds: 300), Duration maxRetryDelay = const Duration(seconds: 30), String isSyncedColumn = 'is_synced', String operationIdColumn = 'op_id', bool autoSyncOnStartup = false, Duration? periodicSyncInterval, bool enableDetailedLogging = false, bool collectMetrics = true, bool validateOnCreation = true, }) factory syncitronConfig.production() factory syncitronConfig.development() factory syncitronConfig.testing() void validate() syncitronConfig copyWith({...}) Map<String, dynamic> toJson() TableConfig const TableConfig({ required String name, required List<String> columns, String primaryKey = 'id', String updatedAtColumn = 'updated_at', String deletedAtColumn = 'deleted_at', String isSyncedColumn = 'is_synced', String operationIdColumn = 'op_id', SyncStrategy strategy = SyncStrategy.serverWins, ConflictResolver? customResolver, }) void validate() SyncCursor const SyncCursor({required DateTime updatedAt, required dynamic primaryKey}) factory SyncCursor.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) Map<String, dynamic> toJson() PullRequest / PullResult const PullRequest({ required String table, required List<String> columns, required String primaryKey, required String updatedAtColumn, SyncCursor? cursor, required int limit, }) const PullResult({ required List<Map<String, dynamic>> records, SyncCursor? nextCursor, }) Strategy & Conflict Resolution SyncStrategy Enum: serverWins · localWins · lastWriteWins · custom ConflictResolution (sealed) Class Constructor Description UseLocal const UseLocal() Keep local record UseRemote const UseRemote(Map data) Use remote record UseMerged const UseMerged(Map data) Use merged record ConflictResolver typedef typedef ConflictResolver = Future<ConflictResolution> Function( Map<String, dynamic> local, Map<String, dynamic> remote, ); SyncOrchestrationStrategy (abstract) Method Description execute(context) Core sync logic — returns SyncSessionMetrics beforeSync(context) Lifecycle hook (default: no-op) afterSync(context, metrics) Lifecycle hook (default: no-op) Built-in: StandardSyncOrchestration · OfflineFirstSyncOrchestration · StrictManualOrchestration · PrioritySyncOrchestration · CompositeSyncOrchestration Exceptions Exception Key Fields syncitronException (sealed) message, cause SyncNetworkException table, statusCode, isOffline SyncAuthException table ConflictResolutionException table, primaryKey SchemaMigrationException table, column LocalStoreException table UnregisteredTableException table EngineConfigurationException message Logging & Metrics Logger (abstract) Method Signature debug void debug(String msg, {Map? context}) info void info(String msg, {Map? context}) warning void warning(String msg, {Map? context, Object? error}) error void error(String msg, {Object? error, StackTrace? st, Map? context}) critical void critical(String msg, {Object? error, StackTrace? st}) log void log(LogEntry entry) Implementations: ConsoleLogger · NoOpLogger · MultiLogger LogLevel enum debug(0) · info(1) · warning(2) · error(3) · critical(4) MetricsCollector (abstract) Method Signature recordTableMetrics void recordTableMetrics(SyncMetrics) recordSessionMetrics void recordSessionMetrics(SyncSessionMetrics) getLastSessionMetrics SyncSessionMetrics? getLastSessionMetrics() Implementations: InMemoryMetricsCollector · NoOpMetricsCollector Remote Adapters RemoteAdapter (abstract) Method Description pull(PullRequest) Fetch remote records since cursor upsert(table, data, idempotencyKey?) Insert or update a single record softDelete(table, pkColumn, id, payload, idempotencyKey?) Soft-delete a record batchUpsert(table, records, pkColumn, idempotencyKeys?) Batch upsert (default: individual fallback) batchSoftDelete(table, pkColumn, records, ...) Batch soft-delete (default: individual fallback) getRealtimeProvider() Returns RealtimeSubscriptionProvider? (default: null) Implementations: SupabaseAdapter · FirebaseFirestoreAdapter · AppwriteAdapter · GraphQLAdapter Local Stores LocalStore (abstract) Method Description ensureSyncColumns(table, updatedAt, deletedAt) Add is_synced/op_id columns if missing readCursor(table) Read last sync cursor writeCursor(table, cursor) Write sync cursor clearCursor(table) Clear sync cursor queryDirty(table) Get records with is_synced = 0 upsertBatch(table, records) Batch insert/update records markAsSynced(table, pkColumn, pk) Set is_synced = 1 setOperationId(table, pkColumn, pk, opId) Set op_id for idempotency markManyAsSynced(table, pkColumn, pks) Batch mark synced setOperationIds(table, pkColumn, opIds) Batch set op_ids findById(table, pkColumn, id) Lookup single record findManyByIds(table, pkColumn, ids) Lookup multiple records Implementations: SqfliteStore · DriftStore · HiveStore · IsarStore Real-Time RealtimeSubscriptionProvider (abstract) Member Signature subscribe Stream<RealtimeChangeEvent> subscribe(String table) isConnected bool get isConnected connectionStatusStream Stream<bool> get connectionStatusStream close Future<void> close() Implementations: SupabaseRealtimeProvider · FirebaseFirestoreRealtimeProvider · AppwriteRealtimeProvider · GraphQLRealtimeProvider RealtimeChangeEvent const RealtimeChangeEvent({ required String table, required RealtimeOperation operation, // insert, update, delete Map<String, dynamic>? record, Map<String, dynamic> metadata = const {}, required DateTime timestamp, }) UI Widgets See UI Widgets page for full parameter tables. Widget Purpose SyncStatusWidget Display sync status from statusStream SyncMetricsCard Metrics card showing pull/push/conflict stats SyncErrorBanner Context-aware error banner with retry/dismiss OfflineIndicator Online/offline chip SyncButton Sync button with loading state SyncStatusPanel All-in-one dashboard Multi-Engine See SyncManager for full API. Diagnostics See Health & Diagnostics for full API. Utilities retry<T> Future<T> retry<T>( Future<T> Function() action, { int retries = 3, Duration initialDelay = const Duration(milliseconds: 300), Duration maxDelay = const Duration(seconds: 30), Logger? logger, }) Retries the given action with exponential backoff. Used internally by SyncEngine for all remote operations. ← Previous Testing Next → Changelog syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License

title: Changelog
content: Changelog Version history v0.5.1 Added syncTableByName(String) to SyncEngine for convenience Removed duplicate HealthCheckResult / HealthStatus from sync_manager.dart Exported sync_manager.dart in barrel file Replaced custom pow() with dart:math in realtime_subscription.dart Fixed RealtimeSubscriptionManager to use syncTableByName Fixed InMemoryMetricsCollector.recordTableMetrics() double-counting bug Fixed SyncManager.syncEngine() using wrong exception type Removed unused clock.dart Fixed README widget API examples to match actual constructors v0.5.0 Batch Operations — 50-100× faster syncs via batchUpsert / batchSoftDelete Sync Orchestration Strategies — 5 built-in strategies (Standard, OfflineFirst, StrictManual, Priority, Composite) Sealed Exception Hierarchy — syncitronException root with 7 specific subtypes Real-Time Subscriptions — All 4 backends (Supabase, Firebase, Appwrite, GraphQL) Multiple Storage Backends — SqfliteStore, DriftStore, HiveStore, IsarStore Multiple Remote Adapters — Supabase, Firebase Firestore, Appwrite, GraphQL SyncManager — Multi-engine coordination Diagnostics — HealthCheckResult, SystemHealth, DiagnosticsProviders UI Widgets — 6 ready-made Flutter widgets Metrics & Logging — SyncMetrics, SyncSessionMetrics, structured LogEntry v0.4.0 Supabase adapter with cursor-based pagination Conflict resolution strategies (ServerWins, LocalWins, LastWriteWins, Custom) Configurable retry with exponential backoff SqfliteStore with sync column auto-migration ← Previous API Reference syncitron v0.5.1 — Enterprise Local-First Sync for Flutter · MIT License
